title: hydrangea macrophylla var. stellata
intro: Hydrangea macrophylla var. stellata, also known as the Bigleaf Hydrangea or Starry Hydrangea, is a type of flowering plant native to East Asia, characterized by its compact growth habit and large clusters of white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to have medicinal benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that are believed to promote overall health and well-being, with health benefits including reduced stress and improved immune function. However, it's essential to note that the efficacy and safety of hydrangea as a medicinal herb have not been extensively scientifically studied, and more research is needed to confirm its benefits.
